color.$description: Semantic color tokens. Never use primitive tokens in UI code — always go through the semantic layer.

background.$description: Surface tokens. default (canvas), alt (elevated), action/accent (fills), danger/danger-alt (error fills).

default.$description: Base canvas #0A0D0A. Use for every page background and full-bleed section. Not for cards or elevated elements.

alt.$description: Elevated surface #1E241E. Use for cards, panels, code blocks, inputs, tooltips. One subtle step above canvas.

action.$description: Interactive fill #4ADE6E. Use for filled primary buttons, selected states, active toggles. Pair with foreground.on-action.

action-hover.$description: Hover fill for primary action elements. 16% darker than background.action. Use for button hover backgrounds when the border matches the background.

action-active.$description: Active/pressed fill for primary action elements. 30% darker than background.action. Use for the moment a button is held down.

disabled.$description: Fill for disabled interactive elements (inputs, buttons, selects). Same value as background.alt — disabled controls blend into elevated surfaces, signalling they are part of the layout but not actionable.

accent-green.$description: Dark green tint #0F2016. Subtle chip/tag fills, callout panels with green emphasis. Pair with foreground.accent-green for text.

accent-blue.$description: Dark blue tint #0D1830. Subtle chip/tag fills, callout panels with blue emphasis. Pair with foreground.accent-blue for text.

accent-violet.$description: Dark violet tint #18102E. Subtle chip/tag fills, callout panels with violet emphasis. Pair with foreground.accent-violet for text.

accent-amber.$description: Dark amber tint #221B08. Subtle chip/tag fills, callout panels with warm amber emphasis. Pair with foreground.accent-amber for text.

accent-indigo-bold.$description: Saturated indigo fill for bold accent surfaces — avatar backgrounds, identity badges. Pair with foreground.accent-on-indigo (white). Contrast vs white: 7.98:1 AAA.

accent-sky-bold.$description: Saturated sky blue fill for bold accent surfaces — avatar backgrounds, identity badges. Pair with foreground.accent-on-sky (white). Contrast vs white: 5.90:1 AA.

accent-green-bold.$description: Saturated emerald fill for bold accent surfaces — avatar backgrounds, identity badges. Pair with foreground.accent-on-green (white on dark fills). Contrast vs white: 5.47:1 AA.

accent-amber-bold.$description: Saturated amber fill for bold accent surfaces — avatar backgrounds, identity badges. Pair with white text (DS debt: accent-on-amber is dark for tints). Contrast vs white: 5.02:1 AA.

danger.$description: Danger fill #C8002E. Use for filled destructive buttons (delete, remove). Always pair with foreground.on-danger (white, 6.01:1 AA). Moved from red.500 2026-07-02: white on #E73027 was 4.33:1 (AA fail); red.600 also aligns the danger fill with decision-engine's.

danger-alt.$description: Subtle danger tint #2A0A0A — dark red for error alert/badge backgrounds, matching the other feedback -alt tints. Pair with foreground.danger (light red) for text. DE overrides to a warm light-red tint.

success.$description: Strong success fill — success badges, status pills, success CTA fills. Pair with foreground.on-success (dark text). Base dark: phosphor green.

success-alt.$description: Subtle success tint — success alert/banner backgrounds. Base dark: deep green tint #0F2016.

warning.$description: Strong warning fill — warning badges, status pills. Pair with foreground.on-warning (dark text).

warning-alt.$description: Subtle warning tint — warning alert/banner backgrounds. Base dark: deep amber tint #221B08.

info.$description: Strong info fill — info badges, status pills. Pair with foreground.on-info (white).

info-alt.$description: Subtle info tint — info alert/banner backgrounds. Base dark: deep blue tint #0D1830.

foreground.$description: Text, icons, and interactive elements. default/alt/muted = reading hierarchy. action/accent = interactive/emphasis. on-* = content on filled backgrounds.

default.$description: Primary text and icons #C8CFC4. Use for headings, body copy, nav labels. Contrast vs background.default: 12.26:1 AAA.

alt.$description: Secondary text and icons #A0A89A. Use for metadata, timestamps, supporting labels. Contrast vs background.default: 7.97:1 AA.

muted.$description: Muted text — secondary labels, captions, timestamps, metadata. Also use for input placeholder text (foreground.disabled exists for disabled element text — placeholder copy on active inputs uses muted, not disabled).

disabled.$description: Text and icons on disabled elements. Deeply receded against the dark canvas — communicates unavailability without being invisible. WCAG contrast exemption applies to disabled text (1.4.3 exception).

action.$description: Interactive text and icons #4ADE6E. Use for links, active nav items, interactive labels. Never for static text. Contrast: 11.13:1 AAA.

accent-green.$description: Phosphor green accent #4ADE6E. Use for emphasis text, icons, and labels that should read in the green family. Pair with background.accent-green for tinted surfaces. Contrast vs background.default: 11.13:1 AAA.

accent-blue.$description: Light blue accent #93C5FD. Use for emphasis text, icons, and labels that should read in the blue family. Pair with background.accent-blue for tinted surfaces. Contrast vs background.default: ~7.5:1 AA.

accent-violet.$description: Light violet accent #C4B5FD. Use for emphasis text, icons, and labels that should read in the violet family. Pair with background.accent-violet for tinted surfaces. Contrast vs background.default: ~7:1 AA.

accent-amber.$description: Warm amber accent #FCD34D. Use for emphasis text, icons, and labels that should read in the warm/amber family. Pair with background.accent-amber for tinted surfaces. Contrast vs background.default: ~12:1 AAA.

on-action.$description: Text/icons on background.action #0A0D0A. Use for labels inside filled interactive components (e.g. primary button). Contrast vs action: 11.13:1 AAA.

danger.$description: Error/destructive text and icons #F87171. Use for delete labels, validation errors, Deny outcomes, and danger alert/badge text. Light red so it reads on the canvas (7.06:1 AAA) and on the dark danger-alt tint (6.62:1 AA). Consistent with the other bright feedback foregrounds.

on-danger.$description: Text/icons on background.danger #FFFFFF. Use inside filled destructive components (e.g. filled delete button). Contrast vs background.danger (red.600): 6.01:1 AA. Never on white or dark canvases.

success.$description: Success text and icons. Base dark: phosphor green #4ADE6E — vivid and legible on dark canvas (11.1:1 AAA).

on-success.$description: Text/icons on success fill backgrounds. Near-black on vivid green — high contrast (11.1:1 AAA).

warning.$description: Warning text and icons. Base dark: amber.400 #FCD34D — bright amber on dark canvas (13.6:1 AAA).

on-warning.$description: Text/icons on warning fill backgrounds. Near-black on amber — always use dark text on amber fills.

info.$description: Info text and icons. Base dark: blue.sky #93C5FD — light blue on dark canvas (10.8:1 AAA).

on-info.$description: Text/icons on info fill backgrounds. White on blue fill.

accent-on-green.$description: Text/icons on green accent tint fills. Near-black on dark theme. For bold green fills, white text is needed — DE overrides to white.

accent-on-blue.$description: Text/icons on blue accent fills. Near-black on dark theme.

accent-on-violet.$description: Text/icons on violet accent fills. Near-black on dark theme.

accent-on-amber.$description: Text/icons on amber accent fills. Near-black on dark theme.

accent-on-indigo.$description: Text/icons on indigo accent fills. White — indigo-700 provides 7.98:1 contrast (AAA).

accent-on-sky.$description: Text/icons on sky accent fills. White — sky-700 provides 5.90:1 contrast (AA).

accent-on-amber-bold.$description: Text/icons on amber bold accent fills (background.accent-amber-bold = amber.600 #B45309). White — 5.02:1 AA contrast. Use for avatar initials, identity badge labels on saturated amber surfaces. Do not confuse with foreground.accent-on-amber (near-black), which is for amber tint backgrounds.

accent-on-green-bold.$description: Text/icons on green-bold accent fills (background.accent-green-bold = emerald.600). White — 5.47:1 AA contrast. Distinct from foreground.accent-on-green (near-black #0A0D0A), which is for the tint background. Use this for avatar initials and identity badges on saturated green surfaces.

border.$description: Edge and separation tokens. Borders recede — they delineate, not decorate.

default.$description: Standard border #1E241E. Use for card edges, input outlines, dividers. Shares value with background.alt but different intent — use border.default for lines, background.alt for filled surfaces.

elevated.$description: Structural chrome borders — headers, card frames, section dividers, prominent UI edges. One step above border-default; not interactive. Use where default borders are too invisible for the component's hierarchy.

hover.$description: Border on interactive elements in hover state. Mid-toned green — clearly visible against the dark canvas, signals interactivity without using the full accent.

active.$description: Border on interactive elements in active/pressed state. Lighter than hover — maximum border prominence for the held-down moment.

action.$description: Border for interactive outlined controls — the secondary (ghost) button. Vivid phosphor green #4ADE6E; a resting accent border is rule-compliant here because the control is interactive. Matches the primary button fill (filled primary vs outlined secondary). Contrast vs background.default: 11.13:1; vs background.alt: 9.02:1.

focus.$description: Focus ring color. Applied as an outline on keyboard-focused interactive elements. Vivid phosphor green — maximum visibility against the dark canvas. Always pair with a non-color indicator (outline offset) for non-color-blind users.

disabled.$description: Border on disabled elements. Recedes to near-canvas — the element visually withdraws. Combine with reduced opacity on the overall component.

muted.$description: Near-invisible hairline border for dense list and table contexts. Base dark: green.chip (#0F2016) — intentionally very subtle; use spacing + background.alt to create separation in dark contexts.
